name: Acrobatics
description: Agility, maneuverability, and gymnastic techniques for movement in combat and bypassing opponents.

novice.description: Allows moving past, away from, or toward an enemy without provoking free attacks, and crossing occupied spaces when there is enough room.

effects.description: Speed check to prevent an enemy's free attacks when moving away from, past, or toward them.
You may cross spaces occupied by an enemy if you can slip through or leap over, but you cannot end your movement there.

adept.description: Grants a quick reaction to being knocked prone and removes the unwieldy quality of combat heels.

effects.description: Speed check to instantly spring to your feet as a reaction after falling prone.
The Adept may use combat heels as if they lacked the unwieldy quality.

master.description: Allows using an enemy as cover, ending movement on them, and gaining advantage on the next attack.

effects.description: Speed check to use an enemy as a shield against incoming attacks they can no longer dodge.
You may end your movement in a space occupied by an enemy if their position allows it.
If you end your movement on an enemy, your next attack this turn is made with advantage.

name: Smoke and Mirrors
description: A fighting technique with short and precise weapons built on hand speed, deception, and surprise.

novice.description: Shifts the hit calculation to Dexterity and enhances attacks with short or precise weapons.

effects.target: Attack chance with short or precise weapons
effects.description: Use Dexterity instead of Accuracy to calculate hit chance with short or precise weapons.

effects.target: Attacks with short or precise weapons
effects.description: Any attack with such weapons is made with advantage.

adept.description: Shifts the Defense calculation to Dexterity and makes defense more controlled.

effects.description: Use Dexterity instead of Speed to calculate Defense.
The Adept decides which of their sides are weak and which are strong.

master.description: Grants a chance to make an extra free attack during your own turn.

effects.description: Dexterity check to gain a chance at an extra free attack during your own turn.

name: Intrigues
description: A courtly combat technique exploiting enemy weaknesses, surprise, and backstabs.

novice.description: Unlocks Backstab when you have advantage and allows attacking through Dexterity.

effects.description: Once per turn, when you have advantage, you may deal a Backstab for +d4 damage.
You may use Dexterity instead of Accuracy to calculate hit chance.

adept.description: Enhances Backstab with persistent bleeding.

effects.description: A Backstab causes bleeding that deals d4 additional damage each turn until the wound is treated.

master.description: Increases Backstab's direct damage and removes the per-turn limit on such strikes.

effects.description: The direct bonus damage of a Backstab is now +d8 instead of +d4.
The Master is no longer limited in the number of Backstabs per turn as long as advantage is maintained.

name: Street Fighting
description: Rough and brutal melee techniques for stunning, knocking down, and countering opponents.

novice.description: Unlocks a dirty hit that stuns the target and allows an immediate follow-up attack.

effects.description: A dirty hit deals d6 damage.
On a hit, the target is stunned.
The stun allows you to immediately make one more normal attack.

adept.description: Allows converting any successful attack into a knockdown.

effects.description: Speed check to make any successful attack end with a trip or shove that knocks the enemy prone.

master.description: Grants a reactive counterattack against any melee attack made against you.

effects.target: Enemy making a melee attack against the user
effects.description: A counterattack as a reaction deals d6 armor-ignoring damage each time the Master is targeted by a melee attack.

name: Knife Mastery
description: Specialization in short cutting and thrusting weapons with emphasis on speed and close-quarters combat.

novice.description: Shifts the attack calculation for short weapons to Speed.

effects.description: Use Speed instead of Accuracy to calculate hit chance with short weapons.

adept.description: Grants an extra knife attack each turn.

effects.description: One extra knife attack per turn.

master.description: Allows forcing close-quarters combat, restricting the enemy's weapon options, and worsening their defense.

effects.value: cannot effectively use other melee weapons; cannot use long weapons
effects.description: After a successful knife attack the enemy can no longer effectively use other melee weapons except knives, and cannot use long weapons at all.
The enemy's defense against these attacks is rolled twice and the worse result is used.

effects.value: initiative win or movement plus successful evade vs free attack
effects.description: The enemy can only disengage by winning initiative or spending movement and successfully evading a free attack.

name: Opportunism
description: A ruthless melee school that makes free attacks an especially dangerous tool of pressure.

novice.description: Allows rerolling failed free attacks against enemies attempting to disengage.

effects.description: A failed free attack check against an enemy leaving melee may be rerolled once.

adept.description: Allows replacing a standard free attack with any other active ability.

effects.description: A free attack can be replaced with any other active ability, but only one success check is allowed.

master.description: Makes free attacks a full platform for active techniques and improves their reliability.

effects.description: The Master may use any active ability during a free attack without the Adept's single-check restriction.
If the hit check on a free attack fails, it may be rerolled.

name: Quick Reflexes
description: Extreme reaction speed and survivability that soften dangerous blows and constantly outpace opponents.

novice.description: Caps especially dangerous damage and negates half-damage from missed hits of such abilities.

effects.target: Abilities dealing more damage than current Survivability
effects.value: half on hit, zero on miss
effects.description: If an ability could deal more damage than your current Survivability, it deals only half damage on a hit and nothing on a miss.

adept.description: Allows instantly slipping behind a missed opponent.

effects.description: After an enemy misses, the Adept may react by swapping places with them and ending up behind their back, without provoking free attacks.

master.description: Guarantees initiative priority unless the opponent has the same mastery.

effects.value: always first unless opposed by same ability
effects.description: The Master always wins initiative and acts first, unless facing another master of the same ability.

name: Quick Hand
description: The art of drawing, swapping, and maintaining weapons with lightning speed, even while moving.

novice.description: Allows beating the enemy on the first draw and reloading a revolver without losing movement.

effects.description: Speed check to draw and make the first attack before the opponent.
A revolver can be reloaded on the move without losing Movement.

adept.description: Allows swapping weapons on the move and reloading rifles just as freely.

effects.description: You can holster one weapon and draw another in a single motion without losing your attack.
A rifle can be reloaded on the move without losing Movement.

master.description: Removes the action cost from consumables and makes firearms more convenient to handle.

effects.description: Elixirs, grenades, poisons, and traps can be used without spending an Action.
All firearms are treated as if they lack the unwieldy quality.

name: Alchemy
description: Potion-brewing and reagent gathering for elixirs, oils, grenades, and special ammunition.

novice.description: Allows gathering ingredients for simple elixirs, working safely with grenades, and brewing combat oils.

effects.description: Intellect check to gather ingredients for one dose of a simple elixir.
The Novice no longer suffers catastrophes when using alchemical grenades.
Unlocks the crafting of combat oils.

adept.description: Allows preparing more complex elixirs and crafting ammunition and grenades.

effects.value: one complex dose or d4 weak doses
effects.description: Intellect check to gather herbs and minerals for one dose of a complex elixir or d4 doses of a weak elixir.
The Adept can produce grenades and special ammunition for bows and firearms.

master.description: Unlocks master-tier recipes and production of heavy weapon ammunition.

effects.value: one master dose or d4 strong or d8 weak doses
effects.description: Intellect check to gather resources for a master elixir, d4 strong doses, or d8 weak doses.
The Master can produce ammunition for artillery and siege weapons.

name: Poisoner
description: Knowledge of poisons and how to reliably deliver them via a weapon or hidden device.

novice.description: Allows quickly coating a weapon with poison and activating it with an Intellect check.

effects.description: The Novice can coat a weapon with poison at any time as a free action.
Intellect check to activate the poison on a successful hit; the poison lasts for one successful hit.

adept.description: Keeps poison on the weapon for the entire battle and speeds up hidden weapon use.

effects.description: Poison applied to a weapon remains effective for the entire battle regardless of hit count.
The Adept can use a hidden weapon in combat as a free action when they have advantage.

master.description: Raises most poisons by one rank, making them considerably harder to resist.

effects.description: Every poison on the weapon is treated as one rank higher, except master-tier poisons.
Poisons at this level allow two Intellect checks instead of one, making them especially difficult to resist.

name: Strangling
description: Silent elimination with a garrote and suffocating alchemical substances.

novice.description: Allows entering a choking grapple when you have advantage, dealing armor-ignoring damage and denying the target's actions.

effects.description: After a successful hit with advantage, you may enter a rear grapple, dealing d6 armor-ignoring damage and preventing the target from taking actions.
Intellect check to determine whether the choking grapple is maintained on the next turn.

adept.description: Unlocks suffocating spores against one target for several turns.

effects.description: A successful throw of suffocating spores deals d4 armor-ignoring damage per turn for d4 turns; the target can still move and act.

master.description: Turns the suffocating spores into a grenade that covers an entire room or small area.

effects.target: All enemies in affected small area
effects.description: A spore bomb deals d4 armor-ignoring damage per turn for d4 turns to all enemies in a room, corridor, or small open space.

name: Oils
description: The skill of extracting maximum protection and useful secondary effects from body oils.

novice.description: Grants natural armor if the character applied a combat oil beforehand and wears no other armor.

effects.description: If you applied a combat oil beforehand and wear no other armor, you gain d4 natural Armor.

adept.description: Allows applying oil during the current turn and extends protection to elements and some mystical damage.

effects.description: Intellect check to apply oil to yourself during the current turn.
Combat oils also protect against elements like clothing and prevent d4 damage from physical mystical effects such as fire, ice, acid, and electricity.

master.description: Removes the action cost of applying oil and allows two body oils to be active at once.

effects.description: Intellect check to apply oil to yourself as a free action.
The Master can have two body oils active simultaneously.

name: Trapping
description: Working with mechanical and alchemical traps, including field construction and disarming.

novice.description: Allows setting and disarming mechanical traps, and quickly improvising them in the field.

effects.description: Intellect check to set or disarm a mechanical trap.
Improvised mechanical traps built in one turn deal up to d6 damage.

adept.description: Adds alchemical trap expertise and increases the damage of improvised mechanisms.

effects.description: Intellect check to set or disarm an alchemical trap.
Improvised mechanical traps built in one turn now deal up to d8 damage.

master.description: Raises all traps by one grade and strengthens improvised constructions.

effects.target: All traps used by the user
effects.description: All of the Master's traps are treated as one grade higher, and master-tier traps allow two checks for their effects.
Improvised traps built in one turn deal up to d10 damage.

name: Animal World
description: Tracker and biological knowledge of humans, beasts, and monsters.

novice.description: Allows identifying weaknesses and traits of living creatures at a glance.

effects.description: Intellect check to understand the special abilities, traits, and physical weaknesses of a person or animal at a glance.

adept.description: Allows choosing a favored prey category and sharing knowledge about it with allies.

effects.description: Choose a creature category; every hit against its members deals +d4 more damage.
If there is time to explain, the Adept can share this knowledge with companions.

master.description: Increases damage against the chosen creature category.

effects.description: The additional damage against the chosen creature category is now +d6.

name: Tactics
description: Combat calculus that replaces speed and precision with cold situational understanding.

novice.description: Shifts initiative to Intellect.

effects.description: Use Intellect instead of Speed to determine initiative.

adept.description: Shifts the Defense calculation to Intellect.

effects.description: Use Intellect instead of Speed to determine your Defense.

master.description: Allows relying on calculation for hit chance with almost any weapon except heavy ones.

effects.description: Use Intellect instead of Accuracy to calculate hit chance with any weapon except heavy ones.

name: Medic
description: Field medicine, surgery, and knowledge of the body for both healing and harm.

novice.description: Provides basic healing without supplies and improves it with medicine.

effects.value: d4 by surgery or d6 with remedies
effects.description: Intellect check to heal a patient for d4 Survivability with surgery alone, or d6 with remedies.

adept.description: Raises healing caps and adds damage from anatomical knowledge.

effects.value: d6 by surgery, d8 with remedies
effects.description: Healing without supplies is capped at d6, and with remedies at d8.

effects.target: Weapon damage dealt by the user
effects.description: Knowledge of the human body increases damage with any weapon by +d2.

master.description: Further raises healing caps and preserves some effect even on failure.

effects.value: d8 by surgery, d10 with remedies
effects.description: Healing without supplies is capped at d8, and with remedies at d10.
Even failed treatment attempts still restore d4 or d6 damage to the patient.

name: Erudite
description: Knowledge of languages, history, and artifacts that helps understand and use the world's heritage.

novice.description: Allows identifying artifacts and reading old human languages.

effects.description: Intellect check to identify an artifact's quality and hidden properties without testing it on yourself.
Intellect check to read and speak rare or old human languages fluently; simple phrases require no check.

adept.description: Allows activating foreign artifacts and speaking elven and demonic languages fluently.

effects.value: Intellect instead of the original attribute
effects.description: Intellect check to activate any artifact instead of the attribute originally required.
Intellect check to speak and read elven and demonic languages fluently; simple phrases require no check.

master.description: Makes Intellect the key to defending against magic, activating scrolls, and reading ancient non-human languages.

effects.description: Use Intellect instead of Will to defend against foreign spells.

effects.target: Spell scrolls, tablets and extinct non-human languages
effects.description: Allows activating spells from scrolls and tablets, and reading and speaking extinct and archaic non-human languages fluently.

name: Naval Warfare and Artillery
description: Operation and organization of cannons, siege engines, and alchemical military equipment.

novice.description: Unlocks work with light artillery and field fortifications.

effects.description: The Novice can use swivel guns, broadside artillery, and construct defensive structures and barricades.
The character no longer suffers catastrophes when using alchemical grenades.

adept.description: Unlocks heavy guns, siege engines, and special ammunition.

effects.target: Cannons, mortars, catapults, forts and ships
effects.description: The Adept can use and organize the construction of cannons, mortars, and catapults, and identify weak points in forts and ships.

effects.value: smoke screens, breach rounds, fire tubes, portable bombards, culverins
effects.description: Unlocks the use of smoke screens, breach rounds, fire tubes, portable bombards, and culverins without catastrophe risk.

master.description: Increases the destructive potential of all military weapons used.

effects.target: Military weapons used by the user
effects.description: All military weapons used by the Master gain the massive quality if they lacked it.

name: Blacksmith
description: Crafting, repairing, and modifying weapons, armor, and ammunition.

novice.description: Allows making simple weapons and ammunition, and fitting or repairing armor.

effects.value: simple ammo, polearms, short weapons, simple weapons
effects.description: Intellect check to craft simple ammunition, polearms, short weapons, and other simple weapons without special modifiers.
Also allows fitting armor to a body type and patching damage.

adept.description: Unlocks nearly any non-mystical weapon and items with one extra property.

effects.value: any non-mystical item with one extra property
effects.description: Intellect check to craft any non-mystical item with one extra property of any complexity.

master.description: Allows removing negatives, adding multiple positive properties, and working with mystical traits.

effects.description: Intellect check to remove negative qualities from an item or craft an item with any number of positive properties.
The Master can add mystical properties to items and produce demiurge hands.

name: Exceptional Attribute
description: Sustained training that breaks the normal cap of one chosen attribute.

novice.description: Raises one chosen attribute by one point.

effects.description: One attribute may be raised by one point.

adept.description: Brings the total increase of the chosen attribute to two points.

effects.description: One attribute may be raised by a total of two points.

master.description: Brings the total increase of the chosen attribute to three points.

effects.description: One attribute may be raised by a total of three points.

name: Sixth Sense
description: Intuitive shooting and orientation without relying on normal vision.

novice.description: Shifts ranged combat accuracy to Vigilance.

effects.description: Use Vigilance instead of Accuracy to calculate ranged weapon hit chance.

adept.description: Shifts initiative and Defense to Vigilance.

effects.description: Use Vigilance instead of Speed to calculate initiative and Defense.

master.description: Allows maintaining full effectiveness in darkness and while blinded.

effects.target: Ability use while blinded or in darkness
effects.description: Darkness or blindness does not affect the Master's abilities.

name: Witchcraft
description: A third eye that lets you see the Shadows, hidden magic, and the true nature of things.

novice.description: Allows seeing the dominant aspect of a target's true nature at the cost of one temporary Corruption.

effects.description: Vigilance check to see the dominant aspect of the true nature of an object, place, or creature, at the cost of one temporary Corruption.

adept.description: Reveals all aspects of a target's Shadow at the cost of d4 temporary Corruption.

effects.description: The Adept can see all Shadow aspects of objects, places, and creatures, at the cost of d4 temporary Corruption.

master.description: Allows clearly seeing the Shadows and the powers linked to them at the cost of d6 temporary Corruption.

effects.description: The Master can clearly see the Shadows of all things and the powers and abilities tied to them, spending d6 temporary Corruption.

name: Marksmanship
description: Pure specialization in damage and penetrating power with ranged weapons.

novice.description: Raises the base damage of the main ranged weapon archetypes.

effects.value: revolver d8, bow and revolver d10, rifle d12
effects.description: Revolver damage increases to d8, bows and revolvers to d10, and rifles to d12.

adept.description: Adds a crippling shot that can pin the target in place.

effects.description: Accuracy check to have a shot pin the enemy to the spot and hold them there until the marksman fails a check.

master.description: Makes all shots armor-piercing.

effects.description: The Master's shots ignore armor.

name: Hunter's Instinct
description: Specialization against one quarry, leaving them little chance to escape alive.

novice.description: Allows marking one target and rerolling failed ranged attacks against them.

effects.description: Mark up to one target; all failed ranged attack checks against them may be rerolled until the fight ends or the target dies.

adept.description: Increases damage against the marked target.

effects.description: All attacks against the chosen target deal +d4 more damage.

master.description: Punishes any movement by the marked target with free ranged attacks.

effects.description: Any attempt by the target to move triggers a free ranged attack; an escape attempt triggers two.

name: Skirmish
description: The art of making ranged weapons dangerous even at close range.

novice.description: Allows responding to a melee hit with a jab from an arrow, bayonet, or barrel.

effects.target: Enemy making a melee attack against the user
effects.description: You can respond to a melee attack against you with a strike from an arrow, bayonet, or revolver barrel for d6 damage, including ammunition effects.

adept.description: Increases ranged weapon damage when used in melee.

effects.description: Ranged weapon damage in melee increases to d8.

master.description: After a successful ranged strike, allows immediately following up with a second free shot.

effects.description: After a successful arrow attack, the Master may immediately make one more free shot at the enemy in the same reaction.

name: Trick Shooting
description: Precise shot control for disarming, blinding, and other spectacular techniques.

novice.description: Allows sacrificing the attack and movement for a precision shot with a powerful secondary effect.

effects.description: You may sacrifice your attack and Movement to perform a precision shot: disarm, blind, pin, or similar effect.
A normal attack alongside such a shot causes the target to lose their next action on hit while they deal with the new problem.

adept.description: Allows hitting by ricochet without direct line of sight against a predictably moving target.

effects.target: Target without direct line of sight
effects.value: if target stayed still or moved normally last turn
effects.description: Direct line of sight is no longer needed if the target did not move or moved normally last turn: arrows and bullets can ricochet and still find them.

master.description: Allows parrying attacks against yourself and allies using a ranged weapon.

effects.description: Accuracy check to parry with a ranged weapon against a melee attack targeting an ally, or a ranged attack against yourself or an ally; a successful parry negates the damage.

name: Suppressive Fire
description: A technique for firing multiple arrows or shots in a single turn.

novice.description: Allows trading movement for a second shot and quickly reloading firearms.

effects.description: By sacrificing movement, you may fire two arrows instead of one — at the same target or different ones.
Firearms can be reloaded as a free action.

adept.description: Removes the movement cost from the second arrow and transfers it to the second firearm shot.

effects.target: Bow and firearm attacks this turn
effects.value: two bow shots without movement loss or two firearm shots with movement cost
effects.description: The Adept can fire two bow shots without sacrificing movement, or two firearm shots at the cost of movement.

master.description: Brings the volley of arrows or shots up to three per turn.

effects.target: Bow and firearm attacks this turn
effects.value: three bow shots without movement loss or three firearm shots with movement cost
effects.description: The Master can fire three bow shots without spending movement, or three firearm shots at the cost of movement.

name: Steel Throw
description: Mastery of thrown weapons and turning almost any object into a projectile.

novice.description: Increases the damage of thrown weapons.

effects.description: Thrown weapon damage increased to d8.

adept.description: Allows throwing twice in one attack and effectively throwing standard melee weapons.

effects.description: Any thrown weapon attack allows two throws at the same or different targets.
The Adept can also effectively throw any melee weapon.

master.description: Turns a single thrown weapon attack into a triple volley.

effects.description: Any thrown weapon attack allows three consecutive throws at the same or different targets.

name: Cloak Dance
description: A dueling technique using a cloak or other fabric as an active defensive tool.

novice.description: A simple cloth in the free hand raises Defense.

effects.target: Defense while holding cloth in off-hand
effects.description: A simple piece of cloth in the off-hand raises Defense by one.

adept.description: Allows blinding an enemy with the cloth and immediately continuing the attack.

effects.description: The Adept may attempt to blind an adjacent enemy with the cloth; on success, the target is blinded until the start of the next turn.
A successful blind grants an immediate second free attack.

master.description: Turns the cloth into a full entangling weapon.

effects.description: The cloth in the Master's hand gains the entangling weapon quality.

name: Blessed Swordsman
description: The highest technique of the balanced sword, focused on counterattacks and pure dueling.

novice.description: Raises main-hand weapon damage when in a dueling stance.

effects.description: Main-hand weapon damage increases to d10.

adept.description: Allows responding with one free attack per turn after a successful Defense.

effects.description: After a successful Defense check, the Adept may make one free attack as a reaction.

master.description: Further increases damage and removes the limit on counterattacks after a successful Defense.

effects.description: Main-hand weapon damage increases to d12.
The Master can make unlimited counterattacks after each successful Defense check.

name: At the Top
description: A combat heel technique that combines kicks and strikes into one rhythm.

novice.description: Enhances combat heels and removes their unwieldy quality.

effects.description: Combat heels deal +d2 more damage.
Combat heels lose the unwieldy quality.

adept.description: Allows adding a free heel kick to other weapon attacks once per turn.

effects.target: Combat heel attack on current turn
effects.description: Once per turn you may make a free combat heel attack, provided no normal heel attack was made this turn.

master.description: Gives combat heels the long quality for interactions.

effects.description: The Master's combat heels gain the long quality in addition to their existing properties.

name: Entanglement
description: Mastery of nets, bolas, whips, and other weapons that first bind, then choke.

novice.description: Allows rerolling failed entangle attempts.

effects.description: The Novice may reroll any failed attempt to entangle an enemy.

adept.description: Allows achieving knockdowns just as reliably.

effects.description: The Adept may reroll any failed attempt to knock an enemy prone.

master.description: Turns every successful entangling weapon hit into a choking throat strike.

effects.description: Every successful entangling weapon attack gains a suffocating effect and deals d6 armor-ignoring damage.

name: Composite Weapon Mastery
description: The art of wielding flails, chain swords, and other composite weapons.

novice.description: Grants composite weapons the entangling quality.

effects.description: All composite weapons gain the entangling quality in addition to their existing properties.

adept.description: Increases the secondary damage of composite weapon hits.

effects.description: Secondary damage from composite weapon hits increases to d8.

master.description: Allows striking all adjacent enemies with a sweeping blow.

effects.description: The Master can attack all surrounding enemies each turn: one attack per enemy.

name: Axe Patterns
description: Specialization in axes combining stunning exchanges and powerful cleaving strikes.

novice.description: Unlocks a combo attack: a blunt side strike followed by a normal attack.

effects.value: d6 blunt hit into normal attack
effects.description: The Novice can first hit with the blunt side of the axe for d6 to shock and stun, then immediately follow up with a normal attack on success.

adept.description: Allows making two attacks per turn at the cost of reduced damage.

effects.description: The Adept can make two attacks per turn, but damage is reduced by -d2.

master.description: Enhances every single strike per action.

effects.description: Any single attack per action deals +d4 more damage.

name: Polearm
description: Classic mastery of long weapons that control range and enemy movement.

novice.description: Increases the damage of spears, halberds, staffs, and any long weapon.

effects.value: spears and halberds d10, staffs d8, all long weapons +d2
effects.description: Spears and halberds deal d10, staffs d8, and all long weapons gain +d2 damage.

adept.description: Allows greeting any approach or retreat with a free attack against enemies without long weapons.

effects.value: as reaction unless enemy also has long weapon
effects.description: The Adept can make a free attack against anyone who approaches or retreats, as long as they are not also armed with a long weapon.

master.description: Turns free attacks with a long weapon into a hard movement stop.

effects.target: Enemy hit by free attack from long weapon
effects.description: Any free attack by the Master instantly stops the enemy's movement, even if the hit does not pierce armor.

effects.target: Enemy trying to approach or retreat
effects.description: As long as the Master continues to successfully meet such movements, the enemy can neither approach nor withdraw without permission.

name: Staff Mastery
description: A variant of long weapon mastery that makes the staff simultaneously a defense, a lever, and a striking tool.

novice.description: Raises Defense with long weapons, and especially with the staff.

effects.description: Defense increases by one with long weapons and by two with the staff.

adept.description: Allows responding with the staff's other end after a blocked attack, and counterattacking after a successful Defense.

effects.target: Blocked first attack with long weapon
effects.description: If the first attack was blocked, the Adept may make a free attack with the other end of the long weapon for up to d6 damage.
After a successful Defense check, the Adept may make a reactive free attack with the staff for d6 damage.

master.description: Allows knocking the enemy down after a hit and immediately following up with an advantage attack.

effects.description: Accuracy check to knock the enemy prone after a successful attack and immediately make a free follow-up attack for d6 with advantage.

name: Double Strike
description: A combat style with two blades or other weapons in both hands.

novice.description: Unlocks the basic dual-wield scheme and a Defense bonus from the off-hand.

effects.value: main-hand any melee d8, off-hand short melee d6
effects.description: You can use any melee weapon in the main hand and a short weapon in the off-hand, making two attacks per turn against one target: d8 and d6.
If you hold a weapon in the off-hand, Defense increases by one.

adept.description: Removes the short weapon restriction from the off-hand.

effects.value: two melee weapons up to d8 each
effects.description: The Adept can use two melee weapons, even if they are not short, each dealing up to d8 damage.

master.description: Raises the damage ceiling for the main-hand weapon.

effects.description: The main-hand weapon can deal up to d10 damage.

name: Iron Body
description: Raw physical power that replaces precision and increases melee damage.

novice.description: Shifts melee hit chance to Strength.

effects.description: Use Strength instead of Accuracy to calculate melee weapon hit chance.

adept.description: Enhances all melee weapon strikes.

effects.description: All melee weapon attacks deal d4 more damage.

master.description: Allows replacing the normal bonus with an exceptionally powerful strike once per turn.

effects.description: Once per turn the Master can deal +d8 damage instead of the usual +d4.

name: Behemoth
description: Specialization in heavy two-handed weapons and their momentum.

novice.description: Raises heavy two-handed weapon damage to d12.

effects.description: Heavy two-handed weapons can deal up to d12 damage.

adept.description: Allows using the reverse momentum of a miss to still hit the target.

effects.description: If an attack missed, the Adept can use the returning momentum of the swing to still hit the target for up to d8 damage.

master.description: Makes heavy two-handed strikes armor-piercing.

effects.description: Heavy two-handed weapon attacks ignore armor.

name: Shields
description: A combat style in which the shield becomes not just defense but also a striking weapon.

novice.description: Enhances the shield and one-handed weapon combination and significantly raises Defense.

effects.value: one-handed swords d10, short one-handed d8
effects.description: One-handed swords in the main hand deal up to d10, short one-handed weapons up to d8.
Shields raise Defense by two.

adept.description: Allows following up every successful hit with a shield strike that may knock the enemy prone.

effects.description: Every successful main-hand weapon attack can be followed by a shield strike that deals d4 damage on success.
Strength check to determine whether the shield strike also knocks the enemy prone.

master.description: Raises shield strike damage to a full offensive value.

effects.description: A shield strike deals up to d8 damage.

name: Unarmed Combat
description: A complete combat art using bare hands, claws, and any natural body weapon.

novice.description: Makes unarmed strikes dangerous and enhances innate natural weapons.

effects.description: Unarmed attacks deal d6 damage.
If the body already has a natural weapon, its effectiveness increases by +d2.

adept.description: Allows making two unarmed attacks instead of one.

effects.description: The Adept of Unarmed Combat can make two attacks instead of one.

master.description: Adds painful strikes targeting the most vulnerable spots.

effects.description: Every Master attack deals an additional d6 damage by targeting the least protected areas of the body.

name: Wrestling
description: Controlling the enemy's body through grapples, throws, and choking holds without magic.

novice.description: After any successful attack, unlocks a grapple with a choice between throwing and holding.

effects.description: Any successful attack unlocks a grapple: Strength check to throw the enemy for d4 armor-ignoring damage and knock them prone, or hold them without damage, preventing movement and actions.

adept.description: Allows counter-grappling after a successful Defense and Speed check.

effects.value: Defense plus Speed, d4 damage and interrupt
effects.description: On successful Defense and Speed checks, the Adept may react by counter-grappling, throwing the enemy prone, dealing d4 damage, and interrupting their turn.

master.description: Allows preempting an attack with a grapple via a Speed check and immediately following up on the fallen target.

effects.description: The Master may replace a Defense check with a Speed check; success automatically throws the enemy prone, deals d6 damage, and interrupts their turn.

effects.target: Prone enemy thrown by the user
effects.description: After a successful preemptive throw, the Master may make a free attack against the fallen enemy.

name: Fury
description: Deliberate battle-rage that sacrifices Defense for devastating melee attacks.

novice.description: Provides a large melee damage boost at the cost of a sharp Defense penalty.

effects.description: Rage allows dealing +d6 more damage in melee.
While this effect is active, Defense drops to 5.

adept.description: Turns rage into a layer of armor and reduces all incoming damage.

effects.description: The Adept takes d4 less damage from all attacks.

master.description: Removes the Defense penalty while keeping all other rage bonuses.

effects.description: Rage no longer affects the Defense score while preserving the ability's other bonuses.

name: Recovery
description: Meditative self-healing that allows getting back on your feet even in combat.

novice.description: Allows restoring d4 Survivability once per day, retrying until success.

effects.description: Will check to restore d4 Survivability once per day; you may retry once per turn as an action until success.

adept.description: Raises the single-use self-healing cap to d6.

effects.description: The Recovery Adept can restore up to d6 Survivability once per day.

master.description: Raises the healing cap to d8 and allows triggering as a reaction to fresh wounds.

effects.description: The Master can restore up to d8 Survivability once per day.
This recovery can be triggered as a reaction to damage just received.

name: Feat of Strength
description: Heroic might that makes the body more durable and dangerous without any magical cost.

novice.description: Shifts the Survivability calculation to Strength, without changing the pain threshold.

effects.description: The Novice's Survivability is calculated as Strength + 5, but the pain threshold does not increase.

adept.description: When at half Survivability, allows rerolling all failed Strength checks.

effects.target: Failed Strength checks at half Survivability
effects.description: When Survivability is reduced to half, you may reroll all failed Strength checks, including Strength-based attacks.

master.description: When at half Survivability, adds bonus melee damage.

effects.target: Melee weapon attacks at half Survivability
effects.description: When the Master's Survivability is reduced to half, all melee weapon attacks deal +d4 more damage.

name: Bodyguard
description: The art of shielding allies and immediately punishing those who strike them.

novice.description: Allows intercepting even an attack the ally cannot defend against.

effects.description: Will check to intercept an attack against an ally that they cannot defend against.

adept.description: Allows defending effectively against all redirected hits.

effects.target: Reflected or intercepted attacks on behalf of an ally
effects.description: The Adept can defend effectively against all redirected attacks on an ally, using their full Defense without restriction.

master.description: Turns any attack on an ally into an opportunity for a free attack.

effects.description: Any attack on an ally triggers a free attack against the attacker from the Master Bodyguard.

name: Soldier
description: Professional armor mastery that makes it a second skin and a mystical foundation.

novice.description: Enhances any worn armor by one protection step.

effects.value: light d6, medium d8, heavy d10
effects.description: Any armor protects +d2 better: light grants d6 instead of d4, medium d8 instead of d6, heavy d10 instead of d8.

adept.description: Removes physical armor restrictions that affect Defense and mobility.

effects.description: Physical armor restrictions that reduce Speed no longer hamper the Adept, who moves in armor as effectively as without it.

master.description: Allows resisting armor bypass and using mystical powers even in cumbersome armor.

effects.target: Armor bypass or armor piercing effects
effects.description: Speed check to counter any armor-bypassing or armor-piercing effects.
Mystical powers are no longer hindered by any worn armor, even the bulkiest.

name: Fortitude
description: Mental discipline and will that protect against restraints and deflect weak psychic attacks.

novice.description: Allows rerolling failed checks to break free from physical restraints.

effects.target: Failed Strength or Will checks against physical restraints
effects.description: The Novice may reroll any failed Strength or Will check to break free from nets, traps, magical ice, and other restraining physical effects.

adept.description: Extends the same reliability to non-physical mental effects.

effects.target: Failed Strength or Will checks against mental effects
effects.description: The Adept may reroll any failed Strength or Will check against confusion, charm, domination, and other non-physical effects.

master.description: Punishes failed mental attacks with a blast of force.

effects.description: Any failed mental attack against the Master triggers a d6 armor-ignoring damage reaction against the attacker.

name: Mystical Powers
description: The foundational ability to wield spells that increase permanent and temporary Corruption.

novice.description: Unlocks a novice-tier spell.

effects.description: Allows wielding a Novice-tier spell.

adept.description: Unlocks an adept-tier spell.

effects.description: Allows wielding an Adept-tier spell.

master.description: Unlocks a master-tier spell.

effects.description: Allows wielding a Master-tier spell.

name: Mystical Tradition: Order
description: The academic Order tradition that lowers the cost of learning and using its own spells.

novice.description: Removes permanent Corruption from novice spells and rituals of the academic tradition.

effects.description: The Order Initiate gains no permanent Corruption when learning Novice-tier spells and rituals of the academic tradition.

adept.description: Caps temporary Corruption from academic magic and removes the permanent cost of adept spells.

effects.description: Casting academic spells and rituals raises temporary Corruption by no more than one point.
The full Order member gains no permanent Corruption when learning adept-tier spells of their tradition.

master.description: Removes the permanent cost of master spells and helps maintain chained and mass magic.

effects.description: The Senior Order Mage gains no permanent Corruption when learning master-tier spells of their tradition.

effects.target: Failed checks for chained or mass spells
effects.description: Failed checks related to the duration or effectiveness of chained and mass spells may be rerolled.

name: Mystical Tradition: Witches' Circle
description: The wild witch tradition that reduces Corruption costs for its curses, natural magic, and rituals.

novice.description: Removes permanent Corruption from novice spells and rituals of the witch tradition.

effects.description: The Witch Apprentice gains no permanent Corruption when learning Novice-tier spells and rituals of the wild tradition.

adept.description: Caps temporary Corruption from wild magic and removes the cost of learning adept spells.

effects.description: Casting wild spells and rituals raises temporary Corruption by no more than one point.
The Witch gains no permanent Corruption when learning Adept-tier spells of their tradition.

master.description: Removes the permanent cost of master-tier spells of the wild tradition.

effects.description: The Elder Witch of the Mists gains no permanent Corruption when learning master-tier spells of their wild tradition.

name: Mystical Tradition: Theurgy
description: The Apoleata Church tradition that reduces the cost of healing and protective magic and strengthens it against monsters.

novice.description: Removes permanent Corruption from novice church spells and rituals.

effects.description: The Apoleata Cleric gains no permanent Corruption when learning novice-tier spells and rituals of the church tradition.

adept.description: Caps temporary Corruption from church magic and removes the cost of learning adept spells.

effects.description: Casting church spells and rituals raises temporary Corruption by no more than one point.
The Brother or Sister of the Apoleata Church gains no permanent Corruption when learning adept-tier spells of their tradition.

master.description: Removes the cost of learning master-tier church magic and enhances healing and holy damage.

effects.description: The Father or Mother of the Apoleata Church gains no permanent Corruption when learning master-tier spells of their tradition.
All healing spells and non-mystical healing practices become +d4 more effective, as do damaging spells against monsters and Vices.

name: Mystical Tradition: Ratassan
description: The Lapalian court tradition of fire and battle magic that reduces the cost of its own school.

novice.description: Removes permanent Corruption from novice spells and rituals of the court tradition.

effects.description: The Lapalian Baron gains no permanent Corruption when learning novice-tier spells and rituals of the court tradition.

adept.description: Caps temporary Corruption from court magic and removes the cost of learning adept spells.

effects.description: Casting court spells and rituals raises temporary Corruption by no more than one point.
The Lapalian Viscount gains no permanent Corruption when learning adept-tier spells of the court tradition.

master.description: Removes the cost of learning master-tier court spells and improves elemental reliability.

effects.description: The Lapalian Marquis gains no permanent Corruption when learning master-tier spells of the court tradition.
The Master may reroll any failed check on elemental spells.

name: Mystical Tradition: Sorcery
description: A sorcerous tradition that does not shun Corruption but learns to use it as fuel and a weapon.

novice.description: Allows minimizing new Corruption to one point through Will.

effects.description: Will check to minimize new Corruption to one point.

adept.description: Allows retrying a failed mystical check at the cost of additional temporary Corruption.

effects.value: gain d4 temporary corruption on successful retry
effects.description: If the sorcerer failed a Mystical Power check, they may retry it at the cost of their own Corruption; on success the spell fires, but the sorcerer gains d4 temporary Corruption.

master.description: Allows using the target's Corruption to lower their defense against spells.

effects.description: The powerful sorcerer can force a corrupted target to defend against their magic with Shadow — Will minus current Corruption; monsters are immune to this.

name: Mystical Tradition: Sirens
description: The song tradition of domination and enchantment, making mind magic especially resilient.

novice.description: Removes permanent Corruption from novice powers and grants a reroll on a single-target mind effect.

effects.description: Learning Novice-tier mystical powers adds no permanent Corruption.
The Siren Novice may reroll a check for a spell or other effect targeting one mind, if it is not a mass effect.

adept.description: Removes the permanent cost of adept powers and allows rerolling any one mind effect check.

effects.description: Learning Adept-tier mystical powers adds no permanent Corruption.

effects.target: One mind-affecting spell or effect check
effects.description: The Siren may reroll any one check for a spell or other mind-affecting effect.

master.description: Removes the permanent cost of master powers and allows repeating mind effect checks as many times as the effect requires.

effects.description: Learning Master-tier mystical powers adds no permanent Corruption.
The Siren may repeat as many spell and mind-affecting effect checks as the effect demands.

name: Mystical Tradition: Symbolism
description: The tradition of magical symbols, preparing and packaging effects in advance without immediate Shadow harm.

novice.description: Requires physically drawing symbols slowly and allows only one to be prepared.

effects.value: one hour, one stored symbol, one-turn activation
effects.description: A rune must have a physical form; its creation takes one hour, only one symbol may be stored at a time, and activation requires a full turn.

adept.description: Allows drawing symbols in one turn, storing several, and triggering them with a word or condition.

effects.value: draw in one turn, multiple stored symbols, free-action activation
effects.description: A symbol can be drawn in one turn, multiple may be stored, and activation can be done as a free action with a short word or a preset trigger.
The Symbolist can erase any symbol with a simple touch.

master.description: Allows drawing and activating symbols directly in the air instantly.

effects.value: draw in air and activate immediately
effects.description: The experienced Symbolist can draw symbols in the air and activate them immediately; such symbols burn in the air until the end of combat.

name: Ritualist
description: The ability to study, identify, and perform long mystical rituals of various traditions.

novice.description: Allows learning one ritual of any tradition.

effects.description: The Novice Ritualist may learn up to one ritual if they find a teacher or other source of knowledge.

adept.description: Raises the known ritual limit to three.

effects.description: The Adept Ritualist may learn up to three rituals.

master.description: Raises the known ritual limit to six.

effects.description: The Master Ritualist may learn up to six rituals.

name: Special Gift
description: A rare mystical talent that helps carry Corruption more safely and access wider magic.

novice.description: Reduces the temporary Corruption of one chosen spell or ability to one point.

effects.target: One chosen spell or ability with temporary corruption cost
effects.description: Choose one available spell or ability: its temporary Corruption on use never exceeds one point.

adept.description: Doubles the Corruption Threshold and Limit.

effects.description: The Special Gift Adept doubles their Corruption Threshold and Corruption Limit.

master.description: Unlocks novice-tier powers from all traditions and allows rerolling mystical checks at the cost of a risk.

effects.description: The Master can use all mystical powers from all traditions at least at the novice level.

effects.value: gain d8 corruption if second roll also fails
effects.description: Any mystical check may be rerolled, but if the second check also fails the character gains d8 Corruption.

name: Guided Sin
description: Manipulating Corruption as your own energy: redirecting, amplifying, and offloading it onto others.

novice.description: Allows absorbing temporary Corruption that was meant to affect a nearby ally.

effects.description: The Novice can redirect onto themselves any temporary Corruption that was about to affect someone nearby.

adept.description: Allows rolling gained Corruption twice and choosing the more desirable outcome.

effects.target: Corruption gain for self or redirected target
effects.description: The Adept may roll Corruption gain checks twice and choose the more desirable amount — higher or lower.

master.description: Allows directing incoming Corruption toward a visible target.

effects.description: Will check to redirect incoming Corruption to a visible target; on failure the Master takes half and the rest dissipates.

name: Demiurge Hands
description: The skill of unlocking the potential of costly mystical hands and more safely redirecting Corruption into them.

novice.description: Makes all demiurge hands flexible.

effects.description: All of the Novice's demiurge hands gain the flexible quality.

adept.description: Allows swapping hands on the move and once per battle redirecting some temporary Corruption into them.

effects.description: The Adept can swap equipped demiurge hands during their turn by spending movement.

effects.target: Temporary corruption received by the user
effects.value: once per battle up to difficulty modifier
effects.description: Once per battle the Adept can redirect into a demiurge hand an amount of received temporary Corruption equal to its difficulty modifier.

master.description: Removes the binding difficulty, allows freely swapping hands, and maintains one effect for the whole day.

effects.description: The binding difficulty modifier no longer applies to the Master.
The Master can swap demiurge hands as a free action.
At the start of the day the Master may make a Will check to maintain the effect of any one demiurge hand for the entire day, regardless of further hand swaps.

name: Runic Tattoos
description: Using ritual-inscribed tattoos as a hidden source of protection, healing, and damage.

novice.description: Allows activating tattoos for temporary armor and weather protection.

effects.value: d4 armor for 1 temporary corruption per attack
effects.description: The Novice may activate runic tattoos to gain a temporary +d4 Armor bonus at the cost of one temporary Corruption per attack received.
Runic tattoos also protect against wind and weather as if they were clothing.

adept.description: Allows converting Corruption into targeted self-healing.

effects.description: The Adept can activate tattoos to restore one point of Survivability at the cost of one temporary Corruption.

master.description: Allows adding mystical damage to every successful attack at the cost of Corruption.

effects.description: The Master can activate tattoos to add +d4 damage to each successful attack, paying temporary Corruption equal to the amount of bonus mystical damage dealt.

name: Leader
description: Charismatic command that turns personal will into spell power and combat coordination.

novice.description: Shifts spell power to Charisma.

effects.description: Use Charisma instead of Will to determine spell power, though the Corruption Threshold is still calculated from Will.

adept.description: Allows designating one priority target for the whole group.

effects.description: The Adept can choose and announce one enemy; all ally attacks against them deal +d4 more damage.

master.description: If a speech was given before battle, shares resistance to magic with listeners.

effects.value: use leader's Charisma instead of own Will for spell defense
effects.description: If the Master had the opportunity to give a speech before the battle, all who heard it may use the Master's Charisma instead of their own Will for spell defense.

name: Dominance
description: An overwhelming presence that makes enemies hesitate, retreat, or end the fight.

novice.description: Shifts melee hit chance to Charisma.

effects.description: Use Charisma instead of Accuracy to calculate melee weapon hit chance.

adept.description: Allows forcing an enemy not to attack the Adept on their next turn.

effects.value: cannot attack user on next turn
effects.description: Charisma check to make an enemy hesitate on their next turn; they will not attack the Adept but may still strike allies.

master.description: Allows breaking the will to continue fighting in an already wounded side.

effects.target: Enemy in ongoing or impending conflict
effects.description: Charisma check to end combat with an enemy: they may surrender, flee, or agree to negotiate; in an ongoing fight this is possible if someone on their side has been wounded.

name: Mobile Combat
description: A mobile fighting style with constant weapon switching and reactive lunges when breaking distance.

novice.description: Allows splitting movement before and after the attack.

effects.description: The Mobile Combat Novice may split their Movement into portions before and after the attack.

adept.description: Removes the limit on weapon swaps per turn.

effects.description: The Adept can swap weapons as many times per turn as desired, instantly switching from swords to a revolver and back.

master.description: Improves the reliability of disengaging and allows responding to incoming free attacks with free attacks of your own.

effects.target: Failed Defense checks against Free Attacks while disengaging
effects.description: The Master may reroll all failed Defense checks against incoming Free Attacks triggered by disengaging.

effects.target: Enemy making a Free Attack against the user
effects.description: Whenever the Master is targeted by such a free attack, they may make a free attack against the opponent using any available weapon.

name: Pyrotechnics
description: Spy alchemy for blinding, stunning, and devastating thrown charges.

novice.description: Unlocks blinding powder and safe use of grenades and mines.

effects.value: d4 armor-ignoring damage and d4 rounds blind
effects.description: Accuracy check to use blinding powder in melee; on success the target takes d4 armor-ignoring damage and is blinded for d4 rounds.
Using alchemical mines or grenades can no longer result in a catastrophic outcome.

adept.description: Unlocks a smoke canister that damages and blinds an area.

effects.description: Accuracy check to correctly deploy a smoke canister; all who do not leave the affected area take d4 armor-ignoring damage and cannot see.

master.description: Grants a thunder ball with high armor-ignoring damage and blinding.

effects.value: d12 on success, d6 on failure, blind d4 rounds
effects.description: Accuracy check to use a thunder ball that deals up to d12 armor-ignoring damage on success or d6 on failure, and blinds for d4 rounds.

name: Artifact Creation
description: Guild mastery of crafting artifacts and reading the mystic properties already bound to them.

novice.description: Allows creating simple artifacts.

effects.description: Intellect check to create a simple artifact.

adept.description: Allows creating powerful artifacts.

effects.description: Intellect check to create a powerful artifact.

master.description: Allows creating legendary artifacts.

effects.description: Intellect check to create a legendary artifact.

name: Armored Mystic
description: A templar school that allows casting in heavy armor and enhancing it with mysticism.

novice.description: Removes medium armor interference with mystical powers.

effects.target: Mystical power use in medium armor
effects.description: Medium armor has no effect on the effectiveness of mystical powers.

adept.description: Extends the same comfort to heavy armor.

effects.target: Mystical power use in heavy armor
effects.description: Heavy armor has no effect on the effectiveness of mystical powers.

master.description: Mystically enhances the armor itself by one additional protection step.

effects.description: The armor's effectiveness is enhanced by mystical energies, providing +d4 more protection.

name: Bloody Combat
description: A brutal combat school of fighting through wounds, making the character more dangerous as Survivability falls.

novice.description: When at half Survivability, allows rerolling melee attack checks.

effects.target: Melee attack checks at half Survivability
effects.description: When the Novice's Survivability is reduced to half, all melee weapon attack checks may be rerolled.

adept.description: When at half Survivability, sharply increases melee damage.

effects.description: When the Adept's Survivability is reduced to half, all melee attacks deal +d8 more damage.

master.description: Converts half of damage dealt into self-healing.

effects.description: Half of the damage the Master deals to enemies is added to their own Survivability as healing.

name: Mystical Tradition: Rune Magic
description: The runic tradition that can store Corruption in a phylactery and preserve power in items.

novice.description: Allows binding the soul to a runic object and redirecting novice-level permanent Corruption into it.

effects.description: The Novice Volkhv can imbue their soul into a chosen runic object; all permanent Corruption from novice-level spells is redirected into that object.
An elemental rune on the phylactery can be activated to increase damage dealt by +d4 fire, ice, acid, or electricity.

adept.description: Redirects permanent Corruption from adept spells into the phylactery and helps reduce new spikes.

effects.description: All permanent Corruption from adept-level spells is redirected into the phylactery.
Each time the Adept gains new Corruption, they may reroll the amount and choose the lower result.

master.description: Redirects master-level permanent Corruption into the staff and removes the temporary cost of using tradition powers.

effects.description: All permanent Corruption from master-level spells is redirected into the staff.
The Master Volkhv gains no temporary Corruption when using their tradition's powers.
